---
title: "Regulation and Remedies"
---

## Learning goals
- Understand regulatory tools (rate-of-return, price-cap, access pricing) and their economic underpinnings.
- Evaluate behavioral vs. structural remedies and monitoring.
- Apply industry-specific insights (energy, airlines, telecom).

## Core topics
- Access pricing (ECPR-style sketches), interconnection, unbundling.
- Incentive regulation: benchmarking, yardstick competition.
- Remedy design: structural divestitures, conduct restrictions, monitors, reporting.
- Evaluating remedy effectiveness with retrospective data.

### Southern African market inquiries and remedy design
- **Private Healthcare Market Inquiry (2014–2019).** The Tribunal-appointed panel ran cost and price benchmarking across eight hospital groups using claims data representing roughly 70% of insured lives. Its recommendation for a Supply-Side Regulator for Health relies on yardstick comparisons of case-mix adjusted costs, illustrating how regulation can be sequenced after an inquiry that already harmonized disparate datasets.
- **Data Services Market Inquiry (2017–2019).** The Commission combined international price benchmarks with operator-level profitability models to push Vodacom and MTN toward 30–50% prepaid price cuts, open-access commitments on wholesale APNs, and zero-rating requirements for public-benefit content. These outcomes show how quasi-regulatory remedies (pricing principles, monitoring trustees) can be embedded within competition settlements.
- **Public Passenger Transport Market Inquiry (2017–2020).** Investigators fused taxi association route maps, bus tender data, and e-hailing trip-level logs to diagnose entry barriers and implicit subsidies. The resulting recommendations—fare-regulation transparency, dedicated subsidy formulas, and data-sharing standards—demonstrate how competition probes in Southern Africa often culminate in regulatory toolkits tailored to informal and formal operators alike.

::: {.callout-note title="Method box"}
- Benchmarking models and simple productivity comparisons.
- Simulation of remedy scenarios using cost/pass-through assumptions.
- Retrospective diff-in-diff on post-remedy outcomes.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Qualitative evidence"}
- Implementation plans, compliance monitoring reports.
- Regulator-stakeholder hearings; public comment synthesis.
- Operational feasibility assessments from engineering/ops teams.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Citations and comparative note"}
- Cite sectoral regulators’ rules and precedent (e.g., FCC, FERC, Ofgem, Ofcom, ACER) and classic regulation texts.
- Include remedy orders/monitor reports as sources; note differences in US vs. EU/UK approaches to behavioral vs. structural remedies.
- Reference benchmarking/yardstick cases (e.g., energy networks) where available.
:::
